title: N-Queens Puzzle on a Rotated Board

statement.markdown: Given an integer n representing the board size, determine the number of distinct configurations of placing n non-attacking queens on a rotated board. A queen can be placed at any position on the board, and a position is considered valid if there is no row, column, or diagonal that contains another queen.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided in text format, consisting of a single integer n.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be an integer representing the number of distinct configurations.
statement.notes: This task involves solving an N-Queens Puzzle on a rotated board with a twist. The board can be rotated to any of its four possible orientations.

examples.explanation: Sample 1: There are four distinct configurations on a rotated board of size 4.

hints.text: Use a backtracking approach to solve the problem. Consider the board as a grid of rows and columns.

editorial.title: Solution Overview
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved using a backtracking approach by considering each possible position on the board and checking for any conflicts.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach would involve checking every possible configuration on the board, which would be inefficient.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the given board size to determine the number of rotations.
editorial.optimalSolution: Apply backtracking with the following steps: place a queen in a row, check for any conflicts in rows, columns, and diagonals; if there are no conflicts, increment the count of configurations.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The approach is correct as it considers all possible configurations and checks for any conflicts using the board size and rotations.
editorial.dryRun: Consider a board size of 4 and apply the backtracking approach to find the number of distinct configurations.
commonMistakes: Failing to consider all possible rotations of the board

starterCode.java: public class Solution {public boolean solve(int n);}
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, n: int) -> int:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: int Solution::solve(int n) { int count = 0; for (int row = 0; row < n; row++) { for (int col = 0; col < n; col++) { if (isValid(row, col)) { count++; } } } return count; } bool Solution::isValid(int row, int col) { for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if ((row - i) == col || (row + i) == col || (row - i) * (row + i) == col * col) { return false; } } return true; }
buggyStarterCode.java: public boolean Solution::solve(int n) { int count = 0; for (int row = 0; row < n; row++) { for (int col = 0; col < n; col++) { if (isValid(row, col)) { count++; } } } return count; } boolean Solution::isValid(int row, int col) { for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if ((row - i) == col || (row + i) == col || (row - i) * (row + i) == col * col) { return false; } } return true; }
